mnemonics.title: Whitney's Theorem Order
mnemonics.content: Kappa (κ) Lambda (λ) Delta (δ) - Remember: K ≤ L ≤ D (in alphabetical order: Kappa comes before Lambda, Lambda before Delta)

mnemonics.title: Edge-Connectivity Values
mnemonics.content: Path: 1 (like 1 foot), Cycle: 2 (2 wheels), Complete: n-1 (almost all), Star: 1 (single center)

mnemonics.title: Bridge = λ = 1
mnemonics.content: Bridge = B.R.I.D.G.E. = Basic (one) Removal Increases Disconnection, Graph Edge. One edge removal = disconnected.

acronyms.expansion: Lambda of G - Edge-connectivity of graph G
acronyms.usage: Measures minimum edges to disconnect G

acronyms.expansion: Kappa of G - Vertex-connectivity of graph G
acronyms.usage: Measures minimum vertices to disconnect G

acronyms.expansion: Delta of G - Minimum degree of graph G
acronyms.usage: Smallest degree of any vertex in G

acronyms.expansion: Edge Cut-Set - Set of edges whose removal disconnects graph
acronyms.usage: Any set of edges forming a cut

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a city map with roads as edges. Edge-connectivity = minimum roads to close to split the city into two parts.
Three concentric circles: κ (innermost, smallest), λ (middle), δ (outermost, largest). κ ≤ λ ≤ δ.
If removing an edge makes islands appear, that edge is a bridge. λ = 1 means at least one bridge exists.
Kn has every vertex connected to every other. Cut one vertex from all others = remove n-1 edges.

analogies.analogy: Like phone lines between cities - edge-connectivity is minimum lines to cut to isolate one city from another.
Single bridge connecting two islands = if destroyed, islands are disconnected. One edge = bridge means λ=1.
Every person knows everyone in Kn. To isolate one person, remove all their n-1 connections.
Circular highway system - need to close at least 2 exits to completely block travel between two points.
